message:
🐛 Add .catch() to eagerly loaded card bundle imports (#2313)

* 🐛 Add .catch() to eagerly loaded card bundle imports

Seven eager import() calls had no .catch() handler, causing
unhandled promise rejections when chunk loading fails (network
errors, ad blockers). Add .catch() to each to prevent console
errors. The lazy() wrappers already handle the error via
Suspense/ErrorBoundary.

Fixes #2190

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Use re-throw pattern instead of undefined-as-never in catch handlers

Replaces .catch(() => undefined as never) with .catch((err) => { throw err })
which is cleaner: it marks the promise as "handled" (preventing the unhandled
rejection) while keeping it rejected so React's error boundary catches it
naturally. Avoids the type-level lie of casting undefined to never.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix card registry integrity test to handle chained .catch() on bundle imports

The AST parser did not recognize import('./path').catch(...) as a bundle
declaration because it only checked for a direct import() CallExpression.
Add unwrapToImportCall() helper that walks through .catch/.then/.finally
chains to find the underlying import() call.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
